title: Lasthenia minor
taxonomy: Lasthenia minor is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is native to the western United States and Baja California. The plant is commonly known as the pygmy goldfields or dwarf goldfields. Lasthenia minor is a small annual herb with yellow flowers, typically growing to a height of 2-10 inches. It is often found growing in grasslands, meadows, and coastal scrub habitats.

intro: Lasthenia minor, also known as Silverpuss or Silver ragwort, is a small, daisy-like annual plant that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
